input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule VE.2.3.3 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: ve.2.3.3
            TITLE: each fire extinguisher must meet the following:

            CONTENT:
            rule VE.2.3.3: Each fire extinguisher must meet the following:
VE.2.3.3 Each fire extinguisher must meet the following:  
a. Capacity: 0.9 kg (2 lbs)  
b. Working Medium:  Dry chemical/dry powder.  Aqueous Film Forming Foam (AFFF) and 
Halon extinguishers and systems are prohibited. 
c. Equipped with a manufacturer installed pressure/charge gauge.  
d. Minimum acceptable ratings:  
• USA, Canada & Brazil: 10BC or 1A 10BC 
• Europe: 34B or 5A 34B  
• Australia: 20BE or 1A 10BE  
e. Extinguishers of larger capacity (higher numerical ratings) are acceptable.

final_answer: VE.2.3.3 Each fire extinguisher must meet the following: a. Capacity: 0.9 kg (2 lbs) b. Working Medium: Dry chemical/dry powder. Aqueous Film Forming Foam (AFFF) and Halon extinguishers and systems are prohibited. c. Equipped with a manufacturer installed pressure/charge gauge. d. Minimum acceptable ratings: • USA, Canada & Brazil: 10BC or 1A 10BC • Europe: 34B or 5A 34B • Australia: 20BE or 1A 10BE e. Extinguishers of larger capacity (higher numerical ratings) are acceptable.
